revision.text:
[[ja:朝日天神山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Hita|qid=Q386968|1=Hita, Ōita}}}} '''Asahi Tenjin-yama Kofun''' (朝日天神山古墳) is a group of {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofuns) located in {{ill|Hita, Ōita|en|Hita, Ōita|qid=Q386968|lt=Hita|1=Hita, Ōita}}, Oita Prefecture, Japan. Also referred to as the '''Asahi Tenjin-yama Kofun Group''' or '''Tenma Kofun (Group)''', it was designated as a [[:en:Oita Prefecture|prefectural]] {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} on March 30, 2004. == Overview == The kofun group consists of two {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds) situated on the southern edge of a forested plateau called Miyahara Plateau, and within the precincts of the Tenma Shrine. === Kofun No. 1 === Kofun No. 1 is a zenpokoenfun with a total length of 33 meters. Its dimensions are as follows: * Rear circular portion: approximately 20 meters in diameter, 4 meters high * Front rectangular portion: 13 meters long, 16 meters wide, 3 meters high A single surrounding moat has been identified around this mound. During the construction of the Tenma Shrine's sanctuary in 1928 (Showa 3), several artifacts were unearthed from Kofun No. 1. These include: * A hōsei (a Japanese-made imitation of a Han-style mirror) variant of a {{ill|Five Beasts Mirror|ja|五獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Beasts Mirror|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135503364|1=Five Beasts Mirror}} * An iron sword * Shell-made decorative beads * Saddle fittings, including mirror plates, gyōba, and tsuji-kanagu * {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} Additionally, a crystal-made miwatama bead was discovered. Similar beads have been found in the {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|en|Imashirozuka Kofun|qid=Q11376795|lt=Imashirozuka Kofun|1=Imashirozuka Kofun}}, presumed to be the burial site of Emperor Keitai, and are rare in Kyushu, indicating the high social status of the individual interred. Based on the artifacts, Kofun No. 1 is believed to have been constructed slightly later than Kofun No. 2, which dates to the early 6th century. === Kofun No. 2 === Kofun No. 2 is a zenpokoenfun with a total length of 63 meters. Its dimensions are: * Rear circular portion: 36 meters in diameter, 5 meters high * Front rectangular portion: 27 meters long, 38 meters wide, 6 meters high Although the mound has suffered significant damage, a double moat surrounds it. This is the largest zenpokoenfun in the Hita and Kusu areas and one of the largest late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} Kofuns in Oita Prefecture. Though the interior remains unexcavated, investigations since 1997 have revealed artifacts from the surrounding moat, including ring-handled jars and large flat-bottomed Sue pottery. These Sue pottery jars are thought to have been substitutes for haniwa jars and are rare relics, with examples observed at the {{ill|Nakanoyama Kofun|en|Nakanoyama Kofun|qid=Q11362767|lt=Nakanoyama Kofun|1=Nakanoyama Kofun}} in the {{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja|さきたま古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakitama Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 埼玉古墳群}}. Based on the artifacts, Kofun No. 2 is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century. == External Links == * [http://bunka.oita-ed.jp/03bunkan/01siteibunkazai/02ken/05shiseki/094asahitenjin/094asahitenjin.htm 094 Asahi Tenjin-yama Kofun] - Oita Prefectural Board of Education {{Japanese-history-stub|qq=}} * [[Tenjinyama Kofun]]** {{ill|Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|朝日天神山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} - {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|qid=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}, {{ill|Hita, Ōita|en|Hita, Ōita|qid=Q386968|lt=Hita, Ōita|1=Hita, Ōita}}, Kobase ... 2 KB (219 words) - 01:09, 15 January * {{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|qid=Q6573294|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)}} | align="center"|[[Asahi Tenjinyama Kofun]]
{{nihongo2|[[:ja:朝日天神山古墳|朝日天神山古墳]]|qq=}}
''Asahi Tenjinyama kofun'' || {{ill|Hita, Ōita|en|Hita, Ōita|qid=Q386968|lt=Hita|1=Hita, Ōita}} || ... 55 KB (6,337 words) - 18:55, 10 January {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|朝日天神山古墳|version=86729795|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:朝日天神山古墳]] [[ja:%E6%9C%9D%E6%97%A5%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] {{wikidata link|Q11517469}} [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:大分県の古墳]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:日田市の歴史]]
